intent: A project management platform designed to automate task prioritization using an AI-driven engine that analyzes historical team velocity and resource capacity to auto-schedule deliverables, effectively eliminating manual backlog grooming and deadline slippage.

entities.name: VelocityModel
entities.description: A persistent representation of the AI-trained heuristic parameters that calculate team output capacity based on historical project metadata.

entities.name: DynamicTaskNode
entities.description: An atomic unit of work containing dependencies, complexity scores, and a temporal state machine for automated scheduling transitions.

entities.name: ResourceAllocationManifest
entities.description: A mapping entity that binds individual human capacity profiles to specific TaskNodes, optimized by the AI engine to prevent bottlenecking.

entities.name: MilestoneConstraint
entities.description: A guardrail entity defining hard delivery dates and project dependencies that restrict the automated scheduler's optimization space.

entities.name: TelemetryEventStream
entities.description: A ledger capturing real-time progress signals and Slack/Teams updates to continuously feed back into the VelocityModel.

entities.name: CapacityForecast
entities.description: A predictive projection entity that snapshots future project health based on the current backlog and historical throughput trends.

architecture.description: The architecture follows an Event-Driven Microservices pattern. Incoming telemetry from external tools via the REST API is normalized into the TelemetryEventStream, triggering an incremental update to the VelocityModel. The AI engine consumes the VelocityModel and current DynamicTaskNodes to calculate an optimized schedule, constrained by MilestoneConstraint entities. The resulting schedule is persisted in the PostgreSQL relational database, and resource mapping is finalized in the ResourceAllocationManifest. The system provides periodic updates back to the UI and notification services, while the CapacityForecast entity is updated asynchronously to provide users with proactive insights on potential deadline slippage.

endpoints.summary: Ingest real-time progress signals into the event stream
validation_rules: source must be a valid string
payload must be a valid JSON object

endpoints.summary: Create a new dynamic task node
validation_rules: state_machine_data must be a valid JSON object
task_node_id must exist in DynamicTaskNode table
due_date must be a valid ISO 8601 string
constraints_logic must be a valid JSON object

endpoints.summary: Get the most recent capacity projection snapshot

rules.description: Ensure VelocityModel update integrity prevents divergent historical trends
rules.logic: If TelemetryEventStream updates show a variance > 20% from the moving average of the last 3 periods, flag for manual review before VelocityModel recalculation.
A transition to 'COMPLETED' is prohibited unless all defined 'DynamicTaskNode.dependencies' have a status of 'COMPLETED' or 'ARCHIVED'.
The sum of effort hours in ResourceAllocationManifest for any resource profile must not exceed the defined daily capacity limit within the CapacityForecast window.
If the AI-optimized schedule predicts a completion date exceeding the MilestoneConstraint delivery date, trigger an immediate recalculation of the CapacityForecast and alert stakeholders.

rules.description: Ensure atomicity between VelocityModel update and CapacityForecast persistence
rules.logic: CapacityForecast must be recomputed within the same database transaction cycle as the VelocityModel update to ensure consistency in UI reporting.
